task.objective: Implement Performance Regression Detection that automatically detects when tests become slower than their historical baseline and displays alerts
task.framework: Next.js 16 with App Router, Recharts, shadcn/ui

existing_patterns.pattern: Database queries use `getQueriesForOrg(organizationId)` wrapper pattern in lib/db.ts
API routes use `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` and auth check first
Components use `"use client"`, useState/useEffect for data fetching
Existing slowest tests card in components/dashboard/slowest-tests-card.tsx for reference
SQL migrations in scripts/ folder with sequential numbering

functional.requirement: Create baseline table to store historical performance data per test
Calculate rolling average baseline from last 30 days of data
Detect regression when current avg exceeds baseline by threshold (default 20%)
Display alerts card showing tests with performance regressions
Show duration trend chart for each regressed test
Allow manual baseline reset from UI

non_functional.requirement: Baseline updates should run as background job, not blocking API requests
All data must be filtered by organization_id (multi-tenancy)

file.description: SQL migration to create performance baselines table
Add TypeScript interfaces for performance regression
Add database functions for performance regression detection
Create API endpoint for performance regressions
Create API endpoint to trigger baseline updates (admin only)

constraints.avoid: Don't run baseline updates synchronously in API requests - use background jobs
Don't alert on tests with fewer than 3 historical runs
Don't include skipped or timedout tests in baseline calculations
constraints.prefer: Use p95 for alerting critical issues, avg for warnings

output_format.code: Implement each file following the code snippets provided
output_format.explanation: Implementation notes: 1. Run migration first to create the baselines table 2. Trigger initial baseline update via POST /api/performance-baselines/update 3. Set up cron job to update baselines daily (recommend: 3am UTC) 4. Add the component to the dashboard page
